dependencies.relationship: Requires all auth and API key endpoints to be functional

task_groups.title: Write tests: CLI structure and global flags (REQ-1)

subtasks.title: Write test: binary structure exposes login and keys subcommands
details: Write Go test in `cmd/afc/main_test.go` or `internal/cli/root_test.go`.
Execute `afc --help` via `exec.Command` and assert exit code 0 and that output contains 'login' and 'keys'.
Run `afc` without subcommand and assert exit code 0 and usage text present.
Assert binary name appears as 'afc' in usage output.

subtasks.title: Write test: --hub-url overrides AF_HUB_URL env var
details: Write a test that starts a request-capturing stub HTTP server on port 9001.
Set AF_HUB_URL to a different URL (e.g. http://env-hub.example.com).
Run `afc keys list --hub-url http://localhost:9001 --api-key test-key`.
Assert the stub at localhost:9001 received the request.
Assert no request was sent to the env-hub address.

subtasks.title: Write test: JSON to stdout, messages to stderr
details: Write a test that captures stdout and stderr separately for `afc keys list`.
Stub hub returns a valid JSON array from the list-keys endpoint.
Assert stdout is valid JSON; assert stderr contains no JSON object/array.
Repeat assertion pattern for `afc keys create` and `afc keys refresh` success paths.

subtasks.title: Write test: non-zero exit code on failure
details: Write a test where the stub hub returns HTTP 500 for all requests.
Run `afc keys list --api-key test-key`.
Assert exit code != 0 and stderr is non-empty.
Verify command terminates within 10 seconds.

subtasks.title: Write edge-case test: missing hub URL configuration
details: Write a test that unsets both AF_HUB_URL and omits --hub-url flag.
Run `afc keys list --api-key myapikey`.
Assert exit code != 0.
Assert stderr contains 'hub', 'url', or 'AF_HUB_URL' (case-insensitive).

checks: All tests in this group compile: `go build ./cmd/afc/... ./internal/cli/...`
Tests are runnable (may fail until implementation): `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -run 'TestBinaryStructure|TestHubURLPrecedence|TestOutputStreams|TestExitCodes|TestMissingHubURL' -v`
Test file references correct package and imports Cobra (`github.com/spf13/cobra`) where applicable

subtasks.title: Write test: login fetches provider list and validates provider
details: Write test in `internal/cli/login_test.go`.
Stub GET /api/v1/auth/providers to return ['github'].
Run `afc login --provider github` async; assert stub received request for that endpoint.
For TS-03-E7: run `afc login --provider gitlab`; assert exit code != 0; assert stderr contains 'gitlab' and 'github'.

subtasks.title: Write test: login starts callback server on random port with redirect_uri
details: Mock `os/exec` browser open call to capture the authorization URL argument.
Assert the URL contains a `redirect_uri` query parameter.
Parse the redirect_uri and assert it starts with 'http://localhost:'.
Assert the port extracted from redirect_uri is in range 1–65535 and is listening.

subtasks.title: Write test: login sends code to POST callback and prints user JSON to stdout
details: Stub POST /api/v1/auth/callback to return a user object {id:'u1', email:'op@example.com'}.
Simulate OAuth redirect to local callback server with ?code=test-code-123.
Assert exit code 0.
Assert stdout is valid JSON and contains expected user fields.

subtasks.title: Write test: callback server is shut down after login completes
details: Run full login flow (success path).
Record the callback port before flow completes.
After process exits, assert the port is no longer listening.
Repeat for failure paths (timeout, provider error, hub error, SIGINT).

subtasks.title: Write edge-case tests: login error conditions
details: TS-03-E2: hub unreachable → assert exit !=0, stderr contains hub URL and connection details.
TS-03-E3: no OAuth redirect within short timeout → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'timeout'/'timed out' and 'retry'/'again', port released.
TS-03-E4: callback arrives with ?error=access_denied → assert exit !=0, stderr contains provider error description, port released.
TS-03-E5: POST /api/v1/auth/callback returns 400 with error envelope → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'Authorization code has expired'.
TS-03-E6: SIGINT sent during callback wait → assert exit !=0, port released, no orphaned processes.

checks: All login test files compile: `go build ./internal/cli/...`
Edge-case tests for all 5 error conditions in 03-REQ-2 are present and compile

task_groups.title: Write tests: API key subcommands (REQ-3 through REQ-6)

subtasks.title: Write tests: keys create — happy path and secret output
details: Write tests in `internal/cli/keys_test.go`.
TS-03-10: stub returns key object with secret; assert authenticated POST sent with workspace_id, label, expires_days=30; assert stdout JSON contains secret.
TS-03-11: assert secret 'super-secret-xyz' appears exactly once in stdout and not in stderr.
TS-03-P2: parameterize over different workspace_id/label/expiry combos to verify invariant holds.

subtasks.title: Write edge-case tests: keys create error conditions
details: TS-03-E8: hub returns 403 for non-member → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'member' or workspace ID.
TS-03-E9: missing --workspace flag → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'workspace'.
TS-03-E10: neither --api-key nor AF_HUB_API_KEY set → assert exit !=0, stderr mentions api-key/credential/auth.

subtasks.title: Write tests: keys list — happy path and HTTP error
details: TS-03-12: stub returns two key objects; assert stdout is valid JSON array; assert each element has key_id, label, workspace_id, expires_at, created_at, revoked fields; assert exit 0.
TS-03-E11: stub returns 401 with error envelope → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'Invalid API key'.

subtasks.title: Write tests: keys refresh — happy path and error conditions
details: TS-03-13: stub returns updated key with 'refreshed-secret-abc'; assert authenticated request sent to refresh endpoint; assert stdout JSON contains new secret; assert exit 0.
TS-03-E12: stub returns 404 → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'Key not found'.
TS-03-E13: no key_id argument → assert exit !=0, stderr references key/argument/required.

subtasks.title: Write tests: keys revoke — happy path and error conditions
details: TS-03-14: stub returns 200; assert revoke request sent; assert stderr contains confirmation ('revok'/'success'/key-id); assert stdout empty; exit 0.
TS-03-E14: stub returns 404 → assert exit !=0, stderr contains 'Key not found'.
TS-03-E15: no key_id argument → assert exit !=0, stderr references key/argument/required.

subtasks.title: Write tests: HTTP error envelope parsing (REQ-7)
details: TS-03-15: stub returns 403 with {error:'forbidden', message:'You do not have access'} → assert exit !=0, stderr contains the message, stdout empty.
TS-03-16: stub returns 502 with plain-text 'Bad Gateway' → assert exit !=0, stderr contains '502' and 'Bad Gateway'.
Write a reusable `parseErrorEnvelope(*http.Response) error` helper test to validate parsing logic independently.

checks: All key subcommand test files compile: `go build ./internal/cli/...`
Tests for REQ-3, REQ-4, REQ-5, REQ-6, REQ-7 are all present and cover both happy paths and each error condition

task_groups.title: Write tests: documentation and smoke tests (REQ-8 and SMOKE)

subtasks.title: Write test: docs/cli.md exists and covers all commands, flags, and env vars
details: Write a Go test in `internal/cli/docs_test.go` that reads `docs/cli.md`.
Assert file is non-empty.
Assert file contains all command names: login, 'keys create', 'keys list', 'keys refresh', 'keys revoke'.
Assert file contains all flags and env vars: --hub-url, AF_HUB_URL, --api-key, AF_HUB_API_KEY.
Assert file contains at least one code block (```) per command section.

subtasks.title: Write test: README.md links to docs/cli.md
details: Read README.md contents in the test.
Assert the string 'docs/cli.md' appears at least once.
Assert the file is non-empty.

subtasks.title: Write smoke test helpers and scaffolding
details: Create `internal/integration/smoke_test.go` with build tag `//go:build integration`.
Implement stub hub server helper that supports registering routes and capturing requests.
Implement `execCapture(t, cmd string, env []string) (stdout, stderr string, exitCode int)` helper.
Implement `getCallbackPort(proc *exec.Cmd) int` and `isPortListening(port int) bool` helpers.
Implement browser-open mock that records the called URL instead of opening a real browser.

checks: All test/doc test files compile: `go build ./internal/cli/... ./internal/integration/...`
Smoke test scaffolding compiles with build tag: `go build -tags integration ./internal/integration/...`

task_groups.title: Implement CLI root command and global flag resolution

subtasks.title: Scaffold cmd/afc/main.go with Cobra root command
details: Create `cmd/afc/main.go` with `package main`.
Instantiate a Cobra `rootCmd` with `Use: "afc"`, `Short` description, and `SilenceUsage: true`.
Register `login` and `keys` subcommands on rootCmd (stubs returning `panic("not implemented")` for now).
Call `rootCmd.Execute()` in `main()`; exit with code 1 on error.

subtasks.title: Implement --hub-url persistent flag and AF_HUB_URL env var resolution
details: Add `--hub-url` as a persistent flag on rootCmd via `rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar`.
Implement `resolveHubURL(flagVal string) (string, error)` that: returns flagVal if non-empty, else reads AF_HUB_URL env var, else returns error.
Call resolveHubURL in each subcommand's RunE; on error write descriptive message to cmd.ErrOrStderr() and return err.
Ensure flag takes precedence over env var.

subtasks.title: Implement stdout/stderr output routing and non-zero exit code handling
details: Create `internal/cli/output.go` with `PrintJSON(w io.Writer, v any) error` using `encoding/json`.
Create `PrintError(w io.Writer, msg string)` that writes to stderr with a newline.
All commands use `cmd.OutOrStdout()` for JSON and `cmd.ErrOrStderr()` for messages.
In main.go, if rootCmd.Execute() returns non-nil error, os.Exit(1).

subtasks.title: Implement HTTP error envelope parser
details: Create `internal/cli/httperr.go`.
Define `type ErrorEnvelope struct { Error string `json:"error"`; Message string `json:"message"` }`.

Implement `parseHTTPError(resp *http.Response) error` that: reads body, attempts JSON unmarshal into ErrorEnvelope, returns fmt.Errorf with Message if successful, else returns fmt.Errorf with status code and raw body as fallback.
All subcommands call parseHTTPError on non-2xx responses.

subtasks.title: Implement provider list fetch and validation
details: Create `internal/cli/login.go` with Cobra `loginCmd`.
Add `--provider` flag defaulting to 'github'.
Implement `fetchProviders(hubURL string) ([]string, error)` using `net/http` GET to `/api/v1/auth/providers`.
On network error, return error including hubURL and connection details.
Validate requested provider is in list; if not, return error listing available providers.

subtasks.title: Implement local callback server on random port with redirect_uri construction
details: Implement `startCallbackServer() (server *http.Server, port int, codeCh <-chan string, errCh <-chan error)` using `net.Listen("tcp", ":0")` for random port.
Handler reads `?code` or `?error` query params and sends result to channel.
Construct redirect_uri as `fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d/callback", port)`.
Open authorization URL in default browser using `os/exec` with `open` (macOS) or `xdg-open` (Linux) via `exec.Command(...).Start()`.
Use `defer server.Shutdown(ctx)` pattern to ensure cleanup.

subtasks.title: Implement OAuth code exchange and user object output
details: Wait for code from callback channel with 5-minute timeout using `time.After`.
Send code to POST /api/v1/auth/callback on hub; handle HTTP errors via parseHTTPError.
On success, marshal and print user object JSON to stdout via PrintJSON.
In all exit paths (success, error, timeout), call server.Shutdown to release port.

subtasks.title: Implement login timeout, provider error, and SIGINT handling
details: On timeout (5-minute select case): shut down callback server, print timeout error with retry suggestion to stderr, return non-zero error.
On provider error (?error param): shut down callback server, print provider's error_description to stderr, return non-zero error.
Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM with `signal.NotifyContext(ctx, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)`; cancel context triggers server shutdown, exit non-zero.
Ensure no orphaned goroutines or open ports in all cases.

checks: 03-REQ-2.1 through 03-REQ-2.E5 acceptance criteria met; 03-PROP-1 invariant (callback server always shut down) verified

task_groups.title: Implement API key subcommands: create and list

subtasks.title: Implement --api-key persistent flag and AF_HUB_API_KEY resolution
details: Create `internal/cli/keys.go` with Cobra `keysCmd` parent command.
Add `--api-key` as a persistent flag on keysCmd.
Implement `resolveAPIKey(flagVal string) (string, error)` that checks flagVal then AF_HUB_API_KEY env var; returns error if both empty.
All key subcommands call resolveAPIKey in RunE; add Authorization header to all hub requests.

subtasks.title: Implement afc keys create subcommand
details: Add `createCmd` under keysCmd with flags: --workspace (required), --label (optional), --expires (0|30|60|90, default 30).
Validate --workspace is provided; if missing print usage error to stderr and return non-zero.
Build JSON request body; POST to hub keys endpoint authenticated with API key.
On success, print full key object JSON (including plaintext secret) to stdout exactly once via PrintJSON.
On error, call parseHTTPError and print to stderr.

subtasks.title: Implement afc keys list subcommand
details: Add `listCmd` under keysCmd.
GET hub keys endpoint authenticated with API key.
On success, print JSON array of key objects to stdout via PrintJSON; assert each object has key_id, label, workspace_id, expires_at, created_at, revoked fields.
On non-2xx, call parseHTTPError and print to stderr; exit non-zero.

checks: Keys create and list tests pass: `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -run 'TestKeysCreate|TestKeysList' -v`
03-REQ-3.1, 03-REQ-3.2, 03-REQ-4.1 and their edge cases acceptance criteria met; 03-PROP-2 invariant (secret printed exactly once) verified

task_groups.title: Implement API key subcommands: refresh and revoke

subtasks.title: Implement afc keys refresh subcommand
details: Add `refreshCmd` under keysCmd accepting one positional argument (key_id).
If no key_id argument provided, print usage error to stderr and return non-zero.
POST to hub refresh endpoint (e.g. /api/v1/keys/{key_id}/refresh) authenticated with API key.
On success, print updated key object JSON (including new plaintext secret) to stdout via PrintJSON.
On non-2xx, call parseHTTPError; print error to stderr; exit non-zero.

subtasks.title: Implement afc keys revoke subcommand
details: Add `revokeCmd` under keysCmd accepting one positional argument (key_id).
If no key_id argument provided, print usage error to stderr and return non-zero.
DELETE to hub revoke endpoint authenticated with API key.
On success, print confirmation message to stderr (not stdout); exit 0.
On non-2xx, call parseHTTPError; print error to stderr; exit non-zero.

subtasks.title: Wire all key subcommands under keysCmd and register on rootCmd
details: In `internal/cli/keys.go`, add createCmd, listCmd, refreshCmd, revokeCmd as children of keysCmd.
In root command setup, add keysCmd as child of rootCmd.
Confirm `afc keys --help` lists all four subcommands.
Ensure --api-key and --hub-url flags are properly inherited from parent commands.

checks: All key subcommand tests pass: `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -run 'TestKeysRefresh|TestKeysRevoke' -v`
Full CLI test suite passes: `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -v`
03-REQ-5, 03-REQ-6 acceptance criteria and edge cases met

task_groups.title: Write CLI documentation and update README

subtasks.title: Create docs/cli.md with complete CLI reference
details: Create `docs/cli.md` at repository root.
Document global flags: --hub-url (and AF_HUB_URL), --api-key (and AF_HUB_API_KEY).
Document each command with a dedicated section: afc login, afc keys create, afc keys list, afc keys refresh, afc keys revoke.
Each section must include: description, flags, environment variables, and at least one usage example in a code block.
Include error behavior notes per command.

subtasks.title: Update README.md to link to docs/cli.md
details: Add a 'CLI Reference' section or link in README.md pointing to `docs/cli.md`.
Ensure the text 'docs/cli.md' appears verbatim in README.md.
Verify documentation tests pass after the file is written.

checks: docs/cli.md exists and is non-empty: `test -s docs/cli.md`

task_groups.title: Integration checkpoint: all unit tests pass

subtasks.title: Run full unit test suite and confirm all tests pass
details: Run `go test ./... -count=1` and confirm zero failures.
Confirm no test skips due to missing stubs or unimplemented functions.
Confirm binary builds cleanly with `go build ./cmd/afc`.
Check for and remove any remaining `panic("not implemented")` stubs.

subtasks.title: Run edge-case and property tests and confirm all pass
details: Run all edge-case tests: `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -run 'TestEdge|TestError' -v`.
Run property tests: `go test ./internal/cli/... -count=1 -run 'TestProp' -v`.
Confirm 03-PROP-1 (callback server always shut down) invariant passes for all 5 login outcome variants.
Confirm 03-PROP-2 (secret printed exactly once) invariant passes for create and refresh.
Confirm 03-PROP-3 (JSON to stdout, messages to stderr) invariant passes for all commands.

subtasks.title: Run linter and confirm no new warnings
details: Run `go vet ./...` and confirm zero warnings.
Run `go build ./...` with `-race` flag to catch race conditions.
Confirm no `// TODO` or `panic("not implemented")` markers remain in production code paths.

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify no stubs remain in production code
details: Audit `cmd/afc/main.go` and `internal/cli/*.go` for any `panic("not implemented")`, bare `return nil` in non-trivial paths, or `// TODO` markers.
Trace 03-PATH-1: verify rootCmd → loginCmd.RunE → fetchProviders → startCallbackServer → openBrowser → wait → POST /api/v1/auth/callback → PrintJSON chain with no stubs.
Trace 03-PATH-2: verify rootCmd → keysCmd → createCmd.RunE → resolveAPIKey → POST hub → PrintJSON; then listCmd.RunE → GET hub → PrintJSON.
Trace 03-PATH-3: verify refreshCmd.RunE → POST hub → PrintJSON; revokeCmd.RunE → DELETE hub → PrintError to stderr.
Trace 03-PATH-4: verify loginCmd timeout branch → server.Shutdown → error to stderr → os.Exit(1).

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across the call chain
details: Confirm parseHTTPError return value is checked by every caller (createCmd, listCmd, refreshCmd, revokeCmd, loginCmd).
Confirm resolveHubURL error is propagated to cmd.ErrOrStderr() and causes non-zero exit.
Confirm resolveAPIKey error is propagated and causes non-zero exit on key commands.
Confirm server.Shutdown error is logged (not swallowed) in the login flow.
Confirm PrintJSON errors are handled and do not silently swallow output failures.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run TS-03-SMOKE-1: full OAuth login flow against stub hub; assert user JSON on stdout, port released, exit 0.
Run TS-03-SMOKE-2: keys create then list against stub hub; assert plaintext secret in create output, JSON array from list, both exit 0.
Run TS-03-SMOKE-3: keys refresh then revoke against stub hub; assert new secret on stdout from refresh, confirmation on stderr from revoke, both exit 0.
Run TS-03-SMOKE-4: login timeout path; assert timeout error and retry suggestion on stderr, port released, exit non-zero.
Execute: `go test -tags integration ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v`

subtasks.title: Verify cross-spec entry point: spec-02 auth/key endpoints called from production code
details: Verify `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` is called from production loginCmd.RunE (not only in tests).
Verify `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is called from production loginCmd.RunE after receiving auth code.
Verify all key endpoints (POST, GET, POST refresh, DELETE) are called from production key subcommand RunE functions.
Confirm stub hub in smoke tests receives all expected requests matching spec-02 endpoint definitions.
Confirm no hardcoded URLs exist — all use resolveHubURL output.

checks: All smoke tests pass: `go test -tags integration ./internal/integration/... -count=1 -v`
Full unit test suite still passes: `go test ./... -count=1`
No stub markers in production code: `grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO' cmd/ internal/cli/ | grep -v _test.go` returns empty
Binary built from clean state runs `afc --help` with exit code 0: `go build -o /tmp/afc ./cmd/afc && /tmp/afc --help`
